skill.name: azure-keyvault-secrets-rust
skill.description: Reference guide for using the Azure Key Vault Secrets SDK in Rust to authenticate, create clients, and manage secret values and properties.
skill.summary: Build Rust workflows for Azure Key Vault secret retrieval, storage, listing, deletion, and metadata updates.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are false positives from Markdown code fences and inline formatting, not executable Ruby or shell logic. Hardcoded URL findings are Azure Key Vault placeholders and official reference links. A medium risk remains because one example prints a secret value to stdout, which can teach unsafe logging behavior.

medium_findings.title: Secret Value Printed to Standard Output
medium_findings.description: The get-secret example retrieves a secret and prints its value. This is dangerous when copied into applications, CI jobs, or debugging sessions because secret values can enter logs or terminal history.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example directly prints the retrieved secret value. The risk is copy-paste exposure rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static Ruby and shell backtick detections refer to Markdown fenced code blocks and inline code formatting. The file contains documentation examples, not executable skill logic that launches commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All listed external command locations are Markdown examples or inline code. No executable wrapper, hook, or script file is present.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The detected URLs are Azure Key Vault placeholders and public documentation references. No evidence found that the skill performs network requests or sends data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL locations are visible documentation text and placeholders. There is no automation that fetches from them.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detection Is a Textual False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged lines do not define cryptographic algorithms or call cryptographic APIs. They contain the skill description and inline Rust helper text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no weak algorithm name or cryptographic implementation at the flagged lines. The high static severity is not supported by file context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret Value Exposure in Example Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation shows a retrieved secret value being printed. Examples should avoid displaying secret material and should confirm retrieval without exposing the value.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit in the example. It is a documentation safety issue with clear impact if copied.

content.user_title: Build Azure Key Vault Secrets in Rust
content.value_statement: Rust developers need reliable examples for Azure Key Vault secret operations. This skill provides focused SDK patterns for authentication, clients, CRUD actions, listing, versions, and RBAC roles.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install the Azure Key Vault Secrets and Azure Identity Rust crates.
Explains client creation with DeveloperToolsCredential and a vault URL.
Provides examples for getting, setting, deleting, and listing secrets.
Shows how to update secret properties such as content type and tags.
Documents version-specific retrieval and common RBAC role assignments.
Links to docs.rs, Azure SDK source, and the crates.io package.
limitations: It is a reference guide, not an executable tool or generated Rust project.
It does not cover certificate, key, or managed HSM APIs.
It does not include complete error handling, retry policy, or test setup.
One example prints a secret value and should be adjusted before production use.

use_cases.title: Add Secret Retrieval
use_cases.description: Use the guide to add Key Vault secret reads to a Rust service that already authenticates with Azure.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Access
use_cases.description: Review credential choices and RBAC role names before deploying a Rust workload with Managed Identity.

use_cases.title: Document Secret Operations
use_cases.description: Create internal examples for setting metadata, listing secret names, and retrieving specific versions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to explain the minimum Rust setup for an Azure Key Vault Secrets client. Include crates, authentication, and vault URL configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Add Get and Set Operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline safe Rust code for getting and setting a Key Vault secret. Do not print secret values.

prompt_templates.title: Review Secret Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a Rust workflow that updates content type and tags, then lists secret names for audit review.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Hardening
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a production checklist for a Rust service using Azure Key Vault Secrets with Managed Identity and RBAC.

output_examples.input: Explain how to retrieve a secret from Azure Key Vault in Rust.
output_examples.output: The response describes creating a SecretClient, authenticating with Azure Identity, calling get_secret, and avoiding secret value logging.

output_examples.input: Show how to organize secrets with metadata.
output_examples.output: The response explains update operations for content type and tags, with notes on filtering and operational naming.

output_examples.input: What permissions do I need for reading and writing secrets?
output_examples.output: The response distinguishes read and list access from full CRUD access using the Key Vault Secrets User and Officer roles.

best_practices: Use Managed Identity in production and least-privilege RBAC roles for each workload.
Avoid printing, logging, or returning raw secret values in generated examples.
Use tags, content type, and secret versions to support operations and audits.
anti_patterns: Do not copy examples that print secret values into production services.
Do not hardcode real vault names, secret values, or credentials in source files.
Do not grant full secret officer permissions when read-only access is enough.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Rust code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown reference with examples for the Azure Key Vault Secrets SDK.

faq.question: Which Rust package does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on azure_security_keyvault_secrets and uses azure_identity for authentication examples.

faq.question: Can it help with production authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It notes DeveloperToolsCredential for development and ManagedIdentityCredential for production.

faq.question: Does it cover secret versions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a pattern for retrieving a specific secret version.

faq.question: Does it cover keys and certificates?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on secrets, not the Key Vault keys or certificates APIs.

faq.question: What should be changed before using the examples?
faq.answer: Remove any secret value printing and replace placeholders with secure runtime configuration.
